text.source_text: इतश्च परमात्मैवेहात्ता भवितुमर्हति ; यत्कारणं प्रकरणमिदं परमात्मनः — ‘ न जायते म्रियते वा विपश्चित् ’ (क. उ. १ । २ । १८) इत्यादि । प्रकृतग्रहणं च न्याय्यम् । ‘ क इत्था वेद यत्र सः ’ इति च दुर्विज्ञानत्वं परमात्मलिङ्गम् ॥ १० ॥
text.transliteration_iast: itaśca paramātmaivehāttā bhavitumarhati ; yatkāraṇaṃ prakaraṇamidaṃ paramātmanaḥ — ‘ na jāyate mriyate vā vipaścit ’ (ka. u. 1 | 2 | 18) ityādi | prakṛtagrahaṇaṃ ca nyāyyam | ‘ ka itthā veda yatra saḥ ’ iti ca durvijñānatvaṃ paramātmaliṅgam || 10 ||

translation.literal_translation: And for this reason too, the eater here must be the Supreme Self: this whole section concerns the Supreme ('the knower is not born, nor dies,' etc.), and taking the already-in-topic referent is methodologically proper. Also, the statement 'who indeed knows where He is?' indicates difficult knowability, which is a mark of the Supreme. (10)
translation.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.10 reinforces the atta-as-Brahman reading by prakarana continuity and durvijnyatva-linga.
translation.interpretive_note: Context-priority and transcendence-marker jointly settle referent.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S02 pass with stable cadence and inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit sattva-kshetrajna and bhoktrtva figurative-usage choices in records 1.2.025-1.2.030.
